message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_unfold autograd via gather-then-scatter backward [bd-hc18]

tensor_unfold extracted values, gathered sliding-window copies in
plain f64, and rebuilt a requires_grad=false leaf — silently
severing autograd. PyTorch's torch.Tensor.unfold IS differentiable.

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with the gather-scatter pattern:
  forward:  result[flat_out] = vals[gather[flat_out]]
  backward: grad_in[gather[flat_out]] += grad_out[flat_out]
The (flat_out -> in_flat) mapping is computed once outside the
closures and captured by both forward (clone for fwd) and backward
(clone for bwd) so the routing is consistent and free of per-call
recomputation.

Overlapping windows (step < size) accumulate gradient via += in
the scatter — that's the standard torch.Tensor.unfold backward
behavior.

Adds unfold_propagates_gradient_via_scatter regression test:
  x = [1, 2, 3, 4], unfold(dim=0, size=3, step=1)
  → out is [[1,2,3], [2,3,4]]
  Under sum loss: grad_x = [1, 2, 2, 1]   (count of windows
                                           covering each position)

Closes frankentorch-hc18. ft-api lib tests: 1164 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
